name: AWS Cognito API
what_it_does: AWS Cognito provides managed user pools (auth) and identity pools (federated identities) — handles user registration, login, MFA, OAuth/OIDC federation, and issues JWT tokens for securing APIs.
use_cases: Managing user registration, login, and MFA for agent-powered applications
Issuing JWT access tokens for agents to authenticate with downstream AWS services
Federating with Google, Facebook, Apple, and SAML identity providers via Cognito
Granting agent users temporary AWS credentials via Identity Pools for direct AWS access
Securing API Gateway endpoints with Cognito Authorizer — zero-config JWT validation
not_for: Teams not on AWS (Auth0, Stytch, or WorkOS for cloud-agnostic auth)
B2B/enterprise SSO scenarios (WorkOS or Okta are more polished for SAML/SCIM)
High-customization auth flows — Cognito's Lambda triggers help but are complex to maintain
best_when: You're building on AWS and need managed user auth with JWT, MFA, and federation — especially if API Gateway is your API layer.
avoid_when: You need slick B2B SSO, rich developer experience, or are not on AWS — the Cognito console and API have rough UX compared to purpose-built auth providers.

auth.notes: Administrative API uses SigV4/IAM. User-facing operations use USER_SRP_AUTH, USER_PASSWORD_AUTH, or CUSTOM_AUTH flows. JWT tokens issued for client apps. Lambda triggers for custom auth flows.

pricing.free_tier_limits: 50,000 MAUs free for User Pools; 10,000 MAUs free for SAML/OIDC federation
pricing.paid_tiers: $0.0055 per MAU above free tier; SAML/OIDC: $0.015 per MAU
pricing.notes: Very cheap for small apps. Costs at scale can be significant for high-MAU apps. MFA via SMS has separate SNS charges.

performance.rate_limits: Varies by API: InitiateAuth 120 TPS; AdminCreateUser 10 TPS; per User Pool limits configurable

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: AWS standard errors but Cognito error messages are notoriously unhelpful — NotAuthorizedException covers many different failure scenarios. Lambda trigger failures return generic errors unless you explicitly format them. Token validation errors are clear.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: AdminCreateUser is idempotent if user exists (returns existing user or error). SignUp is not idempotent — duplicate username returns UsernameExistsException which agents must handle.
known_agent_gotchas: NotAuthorizedException is overloaded — covers wrong password, unconfirmed user, disabled user, and token expiry; agents cannot distinguish without extra context
Server-side auth (AdminInitiateAuth) requires USER_PASSWORD_AUTH flow to be explicitly enabled in User Pool app client settings
JWT access tokens expire in 1 hour (configurable 5min-24hr) — agents must implement refresh token rotation
Custom attributes require 'custom:' prefix in API calls (e.g., custom:role) — easy to miss and causes silent failures
MFA SMS requires SNS topic trust — additional IAM configuration often missed in initial setup causing MFA delivery failures

security_score_components.security_notes: JWT tokens with short expiry. MFA support (TOTP, SMS). Lambda triggers for custom auth. KMS encryption for User Pool data. Advanced Security features (risk detection, compromised credentials). HIPAA, FedRAMP.
